lesson.title: AR Models
lesson.body: An autoregressive model AR(p) expresses x_t as a linear combination of its own past values: x_t = φ₁x_{t-1} + ... + φₚx_{t-p} + ε_t where ε_t is white noise. An AR(p) is stationary if the roots of the characteristic polynomial 1 − φ₁z − ... − φₚzᵖ = 0 all lie outside the unit circle. The Yule-Walker equations relate autocovariances to AR parameters.

rooms.question: An AR(1) process is x_t = φx_{t-1} + ε_t. It is stationary when:
rooms.explanation: AR(1) is stationary iff |φ| < 1. If |φ| = 1, it is a random walk (unit root); if |φ| > 1, it is explosive.

rooms.question: For a stationary AR(1) with parameter φ, the variance is:
rooms.explanation: From Var(x_t) = φ²Var(x_t) + σ²_ε (since ε_t independent of x_{t-1}), solve: Var = σ²_ε/(1−φ²).

rooms.question: The ACF of a stationary AR(1) process decays as:
rooms.explanation: AR(1) ACF: ρ(h) = φʰ — geometric decay. For positive φ all lags positive; negative φ alternates sign.

rooms.question: Order the Yule-Walker estimation steps for AR(1):
items: Compute sample ACF at lags 1 and 0: ρ̂(0)=1, ρ̂(1)
Estimate noise variance: σ̂²_ε = γ̂(0)(1 − φ̂²)
rooms.explanation: Yule-Walker: compute sample autocorrelations, estimate φ from ρ(1), then estimate noise variance, then check stationarity.

rooms.question: The PACF of an AR(p) process cuts off after lag:
rooms.explanation: The partial ACF (PACF) of an AR(p) is exactly zero for lags > p. This is the key diagnostic: PACF cutoff → AR order.

boss.name: The Autoregressive Specter
boss.taunt: Your future is determined by your past. Every step depends on the one before.

questions.question: An AR(2): x_t = 0.5x_{t-1} + 0.3x_{t-2} + ε_t. The mean of this stationary process (given E[ε_t]=0) is:
questions.explanation: Taking expectations: E[x_t] = 0.5E[x_{t-1}] + 0.3E[x_{t-2}] + 0. In stationarity E[x_t] = μ for all t, so μ = 0.8μ → μ = 0.

questions.question: A random walk x_t = x_{t-1} + ε_t has variance:
questions.explanation: x_t = Σε_i, so Var(x_t) = tσ²_ε. The variance grows linearly with time — this is why a random walk is non-stationary.

questions.question: The one-step-ahead forecast for AR(1) given x_t is:
questions.explanation: E[x_{t+1}|x_t] = φE[x_t|x_t] + E[ε_{t+1}|x_t] = φx_t + 0 = φx_t, since ε_{t+1} is independent of the past.
